warnings: This is the second of 2 mechanical sheets in the drawing set (M-02 of 2) -- together with M-01 (page 65) it covers this house's full ventilation-fan/AC layout, split by floor.
GRID: dimension chain matches the upper-floor electrical sheets and the master grid file exactly.

This floor carries all 3 of this house's wall-type AC units (9,000 + 15,000 + 18,000 BTU) and 5 ceiling-fan/exhaust-grille pairs (40+120+40+120+60 CFM) -- the ground floor (M-01, page 65) has none of either, since all 3 bedrooms are upstairs, same pattern as house #3.

GOOD NEWS (contrast with house #3): the largest AC unit here is 18,000 BTU, which IS fully covered by EE-06's AC circuit-sizing table (page 64, covers up to 18,000 BTU/hr) -- unlike house #3, which had a 24,000 BTU unit exceeding its equivalent table's coverage. No spec gap found on this house.

Several exhaust-grille/ceiling-fan pairs are positioned close together near room boundaries (ensuite bathroom vs นอน 3, and the นอน 2/โถง cluster) -- room attribution for those is a best-effort proximity read based on which wet-area icon or room boundary each callout leader line points nearest to, not confirmed against an explicit per-room label; flagged 'room_attribution_by_proximity'.

One exhaust grille callout near แต่งตัว is printed as 'EXHAUST AIR GRILL 6"x6"' without the 'W/INS' (with insulation) suffix used on every other grille on this sheet and on M-01 -- transcribed exactly as printed rather than assumed to be an omission.
Small stair-tread numbers printed near the staircase (two stacked rows of digits) are structural/architectural (tread count for the two flights), not a mechanical element -- excluded from 'elements'.
A dashed 'แนวหลังคา' (roof line) at the bottom of the plan is architectural, not mechanical -- excluded, consistent with the same annotation excluded on EE-04 (page 62).
